channel.title: Press Pause Garage
channel.description: Recent content on Press Pause Garage

item.title: Night One
item.description:
FINDING ENERGY

Work, family life, gestures wildly all around, everything is taking a toll but what’s left still has fingers that want to move, I guess.

I took the very first steps, the simple ones, but they still took time. I got the IDE installed, (JetBrains Rider). I’ve started looking at processes, actually trying to learn and ingest what I’m reading through Claude and simple web search.

Anyways, that’s night one for me, just devlog one from the dev environment-in-progress.

item.title: About
item.description:
I’m a middle-aged dad who builds games in whatever quiet moments the day allows — after bedtime, on weekends, in the margins.

Press Pause Garage is my studio. It’s named for the thing I do constantly: pause whatever I’m playing because someone needs something. A snack. A hug. A ride. Life.

I love 90s racing games. I especially love the era when PC gaming felt raw and unpolished and dangerous — when a game could ship on three floppy disks and still blow your mind.
